data.content:
The build confirms the file is generated (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The problem is that the `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**, so MSBuild doesn't pass it to the C# compiler.

Fix: add the generated file to `@(Compile)` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The compiler doesn't know about the generated file because it's not included in `@(Compile)`.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items within the target using an `ItemGroup`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the target after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about through that item group.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
